questions.prompt: Given the function f(x) = -2sin(3(x - π/4)) + 5, identify the amplitude, period, phase shift, and vertical shift.
options: A) Amplitude: 2, Period: 2π/3, Phase shift: π/4 right, Vertical shift: 5 up
B) Amplitude: -2, Period: 2π/3, Phase shift: π/4 left, Vertical shift: 5 up
C) Amplitude: 2, Period: 3π/2, Phase shift: π/4 right, Vertical shift: 5 down
D) Amplitude: 2, Period: 2π/3, Phase shift: π/4 left, Vertical shift: 5 up
questions.correctAnswer: A) Amplitude: 2, Period: 2π/3, Phase shift: π/4 right, Vertical shift: 5 up
questions.explanation: For a sinusoidal function in the form f(x) = A·sin(B(x - C)) + D: The amplitude is the absolute value of A, so |-2| = 2. The period is 2π/B = 2π/3. The phase shift is C units to the right (note the negative sign in the parentheses indicates the shift direction). The vertical shift is D = 5 units up. The negative sign in front of the amplitude only affects the reflection, not the amplitude value itself.
